title: Add finalizer for OpenBao PushSecret cleanup
description:
# GitHub Issue #193: Add finalizer for OpenBao PushSecret cleanup on CR deletion

**Category**: improvement | **Scope**: Medium

**Description**: Implement a finalizer on the Keystone CR that cleans up PushSecret data written to OpenBao KV-v2 when the CR is deleted. While owned Kubernetes resources (Deployments, Services, Jobs) are garbage-collected via ownerReferences, OpenBao secrets persist indefinitely after CR deletion. The finalizer should delete the relevant KV-v2 paths and then remove itself.

**Motivation**: Deleting a Keystone CR leaves stale secrets in OpenBao including database credentials, fernet keys, and credential keys. These orphaned secrets are a security risk (stale credentials that should no longer exist) and an operational burden (manual cleanup required). This is the standard Kubernetes pattern for external resource lifecycle management.

**Affected Areas**:
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/keystone_controller.go`
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_secrets.go`

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] Finalizer added to Keystone CR on first reconcile
- [ ] On CR deletion, PushSecret-managed paths in OpenBao are cleaned up
- [ ] Finalizer is removed after successful cleanup
- [ ] If OpenBao is unavailable during deletion, the finalizer blocks deletion with a clear status message
- [ ] E2E test verifies OpenBao paths are empty after CR deletion

---
Source: https://github.com/C5C3/forge/issues/193

stories.title: Cluster admin deletes a Keystone CR without leaving OpenBao secrets behind
stories.want: deleting a Keystone CR to automatically purge the KV-v2 paths written by its fernet-keys-backup and credential-keys-backup PushSecrets
stories.so_that: stale cryptographic material does not linger in OpenBao after teardown (CC-0079)
criteria: After kubectl delete keystone <name>, the PushSecret {name}-fernet-keys-backup is NotFound
After kubectl delete keystone <name>, the PushSecret {name}-credential-keys-backup is NotFound
After both PushSecrets are gone, kv-v2/data/openstack/keystone/fernet-keys is absent in OpenBao (bao kv get returns a not-found error)
After both PushSecrets are gone, kv-v2/data/openstack/keystone/credential-keys is absent in OpenBao
The Keystone CR itself is NotFound within a bounded timeout (<= 2 minutes) when OpenBao is healthy

stories.title: Operator developer can trace OpenBao finalizer lifecycle via conditions and events
stories.want: the controller to add the OpenBao finalizer on first reconcile and emit Events when cleanup starts and completes
stories.so_that: finalizer state is observable via kubectl describe without reading controller source (CC-0079)
criteria: After first reconcile, metadata.finalizers contains "keystone.openstack.c5c3.io/openbao-finalizer"
When the CR enters Terminating state, a Normal Event with reason "FinalizingOpenBaoSecrets" is emitted exactly once
When OpenBao cleanup completes, a Normal Event with reason "OpenBaoSecretsFinalized" is emitted exactly once
All finalizer-related log lines include keystone.name and keystone.namespace via WithValues

stories.title: SRE sees a clear status message when OpenBao is unavailable during deletion
stories.want: the Keystone CR's conditions to surface a descriptive message while ESO is blocked cleaning up OpenBao
stories.so_that: a stuck deletion can be diagnosed from kubectl get keystone -o yaml without attaching a debugger (CC-0079)
criteria: While any backup PushSecret is still present after the finalizer has issued Delete, status.conditions contains SecretsReady=False with reason="OpenBaoFinalizerBlocked"
The condition message names the PushSecret that is still Terminating and mentions OpenBao
The condition clears (SecretsReady becomes absent alongside the CR itself) once both PushSecrets are NotFound
Reconcile is retried with RequeueSecretPolling while deletion is blocked so the condition message stays fresh

stories.title: Finalizer handler tolerates already-deleted PushSecrets
stories.want: cleanup to succeed when fernet-keys-backup or credential-keys-backup PushSecrets have already been removed by another actor
stories.so_that: re-running the finalizer after operator restart, retry, or manual intervention never blocks CR removal (CC-0079)
criteria: If fernet-keys-backup PushSecret is already NotFound, Delete is treated as success
If credential-keys-backup PushSecret is already NotFound, Delete is treated as success
Reconcile returns no error and proceeds to remove the finalizer when both PushSecrets are absent
The finalizer handler is idempotent: calling it twice in a row produces the same outcome without duplicate Delete API calls
No error Event is emitted for NotFound responses

stories.title: Existing Keystone CRs without the OpenBao finalizer are upgraded transparently
stories.want: Keystone CRs created before this change to gain the finalizer on their next reconcile
stories.so_that: upgrading the operator does not require manual migration of existing CRs (CC-0079)
criteria: A CR reconciled by the old controller (no openbao finalizer) receives the finalizer on its first reconcile under the new controller
The finalizer addition triggers a single extra reconcile (requeue) and then proceeds normally
The CR's status and spec are otherwise unchanged by the finalizer addition itself
Deletion of an upgraded CR triggers the full OpenBao cleanup flow

stories.title: Terminating CRs skip sub-reconcilers so PushSecrets are not recreated during cleanup
stories.want: the Reconcile entry point to short-circuit to the finalizer handler when DeletionTimestamp is non-zero
stories.so_that: reconcileFernetKeys and reconcileCredentialKeys do not re-create the PushSecrets the finalizer is deleting (CC-0079)
criteria: Reconcile observed with DeletionTimestamp != 0 never invokes reconcileSecrets, reconcileConfig, reconcileFernetKeys, reconcileCredentialKeys, reconcileDatabase, reconcilePolicyValidation, reconcileDeployment, reconcileHealthCheck, reconcileHPA, reconcileBootstrap, or reconcileTrustFlush
No Deployment, Service, ConfigMap, PushSecret, or Job is created during a terminating reconcile
The existing happy-path Reconcile behaviour is unchanged for live CRs (no regression in CC-0013 / CC-0073 tests)

stories.title: E2E chainsaw test validates end-to-end deletion of OpenBao material
stories.want: tests/e2e/keystone/deletion-cleanup/chainsaw-test.yaml to assert finalizer presence and KV-v2 path absence after deletion
stories.so_that: regressions in finalizer behaviour are caught in CI before release (CC-0079)
criteria: The existing Ready=True step asserts metadata.finalizers contains "keystone.openstack.c5c3.io/openbao-finalizer"
After CR deletion, the test asserts both backup PushSecret CRs are NotFound
After CR deletion, a script step uses kubectl exec against the openbao StatefulSet to run bao kv get and asserts non-zero exit (path absent) for both KV-v2 paths
The deletion step uses a bounded timeout (<= 2 minutes) and fails if the CR remains in Terminating state

stories.title: Documentation reader understands the OpenBao finalizer contract
stories.want: docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md to describe the finalizer name, lifecycle, cleanup scope, and failure modes
stories.so_that: future contributors and SREs can reason about OpenBao teardown without reading controller source (CC-0079)
criteria: Reference doc lists the finalizer constant name and the KV-v2 paths it covers
Reference doc describes the DeletionPolicy=Delete setting on the two backup PushSecrets and how ESO performs the remote delete
Reference doc documents graceful NotFound handling, idempotency, and the SecretsReady=False/OpenBaoFinalizerBlocked condition
Reference doc references both fernet-keys-backup and credential-keys-backup by name

requirements.description: The KeystoneReconciler SHALL add a finalizer named "keystone.openstack.c5c3.io/openbao-finalizer" to every Keystone CR on its first reconcile when the CR is not being deleted (CC-0079)
requirements.rationale: The finalizer is the hook that guarantees ordered cleanup of OpenBao KV-v2 paths before the Keystone CR disappears from etcd

scenarios.name: New CR gains finalizer
scenarios.when: a Keystone CR without the openbao finalizer is reconciled and DeletionTimestamp is zero
scenarios.then: the controller appends the finalizer via controllerutil.AddFinalizer, issues client.Update, and returns ctrl.Result{Requeue: true}
and_then: the CR observed after the reconcile has metadata.finalizers containing the finalizer constant
no sub-reconciler is invoked on this initial pass once the finalizer has been patched

scenarios.name: CR with finalizer is reconciled normally
scenarios.when: a Keystone CR already containing the openbao finalizer is reconciled and DeletionTimestamp is zero
scenarios.then: the controller does not modify metadata.finalizers and proceeds to the sub-reconciler pipeline
and_then: no extra Update call is issued when the finalizer is already present

scenarios.name: Update conflict is surfaced for retry
scenarios.when: the finalizer Update call returns a Conflict error due to stale resourceVersion
scenarios.then: Reconcile returns the error so controller-runtime requeues with backoff
and_then: no partial state is persisted
no Event is emitted for this transient error

requirements.description: When a Keystone CR is being deleted (metadata.DeletionTimestamp non-zero) and the openbao finalizer is present, the controller SHALL delete the {name}-fernet-keys-backup and {name}-credential-keys-backup PushSecret CRs before removing the finalizer (CC-0079)
requirements.rationale: Deterministic deletion of the PushSecret CRs (with DeletionPolicy=Delete) drives ESO to purge the KV-v2 paths in OpenBao, satisfying the feature's cleanup contract

scenarios.name: Both PushSecrets present and deleted
scenarios.when: a Keystone CR enters Terminating state with both backup PushSecret CRs still live
scenarios.then: the finalizer handler issues client.Delete(IgnoreNotFound) for each PushSecret and returns done=false until a subsequent Get confirms both are NotFound
and_then: when both are NotFound, the handler removes the finalizer via controllerutil.RemoveFinalizer and client.Update
a Normal Event with reason "OpenBaoSecretsFinalized" is recorded on the Keystone CR before the Update

scenarios.name: Finalizer absent on terminating CR is a no-op
scenarios.when: a Keystone CR that never had the openbao finalizer (e.g. force-patched) enters Terminating state
scenarios.then: Reconcile returns ctrl.Result{} without attempting PushSecret cleanup and without removing a finalizer it does not own
and_then: the CR is garbage-collected by the API server once any remaining finalizers (foreign) are also cleared
no Event is emitted

scenarios.name: Cleanup completes in a single reconcile when PushSecrets already absent
scenarios.when: a Terminating CR is reconciled and both backup PushSecrets are already NotFound (prior run or manual deletion)
scenarios.then: finalizeOpenBaoSecrets returns done=true on the first call and the finalizer is removed in the same reconcile pass
and_then: exactly one Normal Event with reason "OpenBaoSecretsFinalized" is emitted

requirements.description: The finalizer handler SHALL treat apierrors.IsNotFound responses from client.Delete and client.Get on backup PushSecrets as success and SHALL NOT propagate them as reconciler errors (CC-0079)
requirements.rationale: The backup PushSecrets may already be gone (external deletion, owner-ref garbage collection, previous finalizer pass) and must not block Keystone CR removal

scenarios.name: fernet-keys-backup already gone
scenarios.when: the finalizer issues Delete on {name}-fernet-keys-backup and receives NotFound
scenarios.then: the handler proceeds to credential-keys-backup without returning an error
and_then: no error Event is emitted
the skipped delete is recorded at V(1) debug level only

scenarios.name: credential-keys-backup already gone mid-flight
scenarios.when: fernet-keys-backup Delete succeeds but credential-keys-backup was deleted externally between reconcile calls
scenarios.then: the credential-keys-backup Delete returns NotFound and the handler treats the resource as already cleaned up

scenarios.name: Both PushSecrets already gone on entry
scenarios.when: the handler is re-invoked after a successful previous cleanup and both PushSecrets are NotFound on the initial Get
scenarios.then: the handler removes the finalizer and returns ctrl.Result{} without error
and_then: exactly one Normal "OpenBaoSecretsFinalized" Event is still emitted to mark completion

requirements.description: While either backup PushSecret still exists after the finalizer has issued Delete, the finalizer handler SHALL requeue using RequeueSecretPolling and SHALL NOT remove the openbao finalizer (CC-0079)
requirements.rationale: ESO owns the PushSecret deletion flow (remove remote KV-v2 path, then remove its own finalizer); the Keystone operator must wait for actual object removal before declaring OpenBao cleanup complete

scenarios.name: fernet-keys-backup still terminating
scenarios.when: Delete on {name}-fernet-keys-backup succeeds but a subsequent Get still returns the PushSecret with DeletionTimestamp set (ESO finalizer still present)
scenarios.then: Reconcile returns ctrl.Result{RequeueAfter: RequeueSecretPolling}, nil without removing the openbao finalizer
and_then: status.conditions contains SecretsReady=False with reason="OpenBaoFinalizerBlocked" and a message naming the PushSecret
the log line "waiting for PushSecret to be deleted by ESO" is recorded at Info level

scenarios.name: credential-keys-backup still terminating
scenarios.when: fernet-keys-backup is NotFound but credential-keys-backup is still Terminating
scenarios.then: Reconcile returns a RequeueAfter without removing the finalizer
and_then: the SecretsReady=False condition message names credential-keys-backup

scenarios.name: OpenBao unreachable causes ESO to stall
scenarios.when: ESO cannot reach OpenBao and leaves its own finalizer on the PushSecret for > 30 seconds
scenarios.then: the Keystone reconciler keeps requeueing with RequeueSecretPolling and surfaces SecretsReady=False/OpenBaoFinalizerBlocked every reconcile
and_then: no timeout escalation occurs in the Keystone operator itself; it relies on ESO to progress
kubectl describe keystone shows the stuck condition message

requirements.description: The openbao finalizer constant SHALL be defined as a package-level string in operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_secrets.go with the value "keystone.openstack.c5c3.io/openbao-finalizer" (CC-0079)
requirements.rationale: A single constant prevents drift between Reconcile, the finalizer handler, unit tests, chainsaw tests, and documentation

scenarios.name: Constant defined once in controller code
scenarios.when: the codebase is grepped for the finalizer name
scenarios.then: the literal string appears in exactly one Go file (reconcile_secrets.go) plus the chainsaw test YAML and the reference doc
and_then: no duplicate string literals exist inside the operators/keystone Go packages

scenarios.name: Constant uses canonical CRD group prefix
scenarios.when: the finalizer name is validated against the Keystone CRD group "keystone.openstack.c5c3.io"
scenarios.then: the finalizer string starts with that group prefix
and_then: kubectl get keystone -o yaml displays the finalizer under the canonical group

scenarios.name: Constant is referenced from tests
scenarios.when: unit, integration, and chainsaw tests assert finalizer presence
scenarios.then: the Go tests import and compare against the package constant rather than a string literal

requirements.description: The existing Reconcile entry point SHALL be updated so that finalizer handling executes after the CR fetch and before any sub-reconciler runs, and so that a Terminating CR never invokes sub-reconcilers (CC-0079)
requirements.rationale: Running sub-reconcilers on a Terminating CR would re-create the backup PushSecrets that the finalizer is deleting, causing an infinite loop and defeating the cleanup contract

scenarios.name: Terminating CR skips sub-reconcilers
scenarios.when: Reconcile observes a CR with DeletionTimestamp != 0
scenarios.then: reconcileSecrets, reconcileConfig, reconcileFernetKeys, reconcileCredentialKeys, reconcileDatabase, reconcilePolicyValidation, reconcileDeployment, reconcileHealthCheck, reconcileHPA, reconcileBootstrap, and reconcileTrustFlush are NOT invoked
and_then: control flow goes directly to the finalizer handler
no Deployment/ConfigMap/Job/PushSecret is created during the terminating reconcile

scenarios.name: Non-terminating CR without finalizer triggers requeue
scenarios.when: Reconcile observes a live CR missing the openbao finalizer
scenarios.then: the finalizer is added via Update and the function returns ctrl.Result{Requeue: true} without running sub-reconcilers in the same pass
and_then: the next reconcile sees the finalizer already present and proceeds normally

scenarios.name: Live CR with finalizer runs the normal pipeline
scenarios.when: Reconcile observes a live CR that already contains the openbao finalizer
scenarios.then: the existing sub-reconciler pipeline executes unchanged
and_then: no behavioural regression in the happy path compared to pre-CC-0079 behaviour

requirements.description: The controller SHALL emit Kubernetes Events on finalizer lifecycle transitions: Normal "FinalizingOpenBaoSecrets" when cleanup starts, Normal "OpenBaoSecretsFinalized" when it completes (CC-0079)
requirements.rationale: SREs rely on kubectl describe keystone to understand why a CR is Terminating and whether OpenBao cleanup is progressing

scenarios.name: Start event on first terminating reconcile
scenarios.when: the controller first observes DeletionTimestamp != 0 on a CR with the openbao finalizer and at least one backup PushSecret still present
scenarios.then: a Normal Event with reason "FinalizingOpenBaoSecrets" and a message naming the Keystone CR is recorded
and_then: subsequent reconciles during the same termination do NOT re-emit the start event

scenarios.name: Completion event on finalizer removal
scenarios.when: both backup PushSecrets are confirmed NotFound and the controller is about to remove the openbao finalizer
scenarios.then: a Normal Event with reason "OpenBaoSecretsFinalized" and a summary of cleaned-up KV-v2 paths is recorded before the Update
and_then: the event contains at minimum the Keystone CR name

scenarios.name: No event spam on retry
scenarios.when: a transient error or RequeueAfter causes the reconcile to retry while PushSecrets are still Terminating
scenarios.then: no additional start/completion Events are emitted for the retry path
and_then: only one start and one completion Event appear in kubectl describe output per deletion

requirements.description: The fernetKeysPushSecret and credentialKeysPushSecret builders SHALL set Spec.DeletionPolicy to esov1alpha1.PushSecretDeletionPolicyDelete so that deleting the PushSecret CR causes ESO to purge the corresponding KV-v2 path in OpenBao (CC-0079)
requirements.rationale: The Keystone operator does not hold OpenBao credentials; ESO is the only component authorised to write/delete the KV-v2 paths, so the cleanup must flow through ESO's DeletionPolicy mechanism

scenarios.name: Fernet backup PushSecret has DeletionPolicy=Delete
scenarios.when: reconcileFernetKeys creates or updates the {name}-fernet-keys-backup PushSecret
scenarios.then: Spec.DeletionPolicy equals esov1alpha1.PushSecretDeletionPolicyDelete
and_then: kubectl get pushsecret {name}-fernet-keys-backup -o yaml shows deletionPolicy: Delete

scenarios.name: Credential backup PushSecret has DeletionPolicy=Delete
scenarios.when: reconcileCredentialKeys creates or updates the {name}-credential-keys-backup PushSecret
scenarios.then: Spec.DeletionPolicy equals esov1alpha1.PushSecretDeletionPolicyDelete
and_then: kubectl get pushsecret {name}-credential-keys-backup -o yaml shows deletionPolicy: Delete

scenarios.name: EnsurePushSecret treats DeletionPolicy as part of the spec it reconciles
scenarios.when: an existing PushSecret has a drifted deletionPolicy value
scenarios.then: EnsurePushSecret updates the spec to include DeletionPolicy=Delete via the existing DeepEqual drift-detection path
and_then: no additional reconciler code is required beyond setting the field in the builder

requirements.description: While the openbao finalizer is blocked on ESO, the controller SHALL set the SecretsReady condition to False with reason "OpenBaoFinalizerBlocked" and a message identifying the PushSecret that is still Terminating (CC-0079)
requirements.rationale: The existing SecretsReady condition is the standard surface for secret-store lifecycle issues (CC-0047); reusing it keeps status coherent and surfaces the blocker via kubectl describe

scenarios.name: Condition set when fernet-keys-backup stuck
scenarios.when: fernet-keys-backup is still Terminating after Delete
scenarios.then: status.conditions contains SecretsReady=False, reason="OpenBaoFinalizerBlocked", message including the literal "fernet-keys-backup" and "OpenBao"
and_then: ObservedGeneration equals keystone.Generation on the condition

scenarios.name: Condition set when credential-keys-backup stuck
scenarios.when: credential-keys-backup is still Terminating after Delete
scenarios.then: status.conditions contains SecretsReady=False, reason="OpenBaoFinalizerBlocked", message including the literal "credential-keys-backup"

scenarios.name: Condition not set when cleanup completes immediately
scenarios.when: both PushSecrets are NotFound on the first Get during the terminating reconcile
scenarios.then: no blocker condition is written because the finalizer is removed in the same pass
and_then: status.conditions does not contain reason="OpenBaoFinalizerBlocked"

requirements.description: The E2E chainsaw test tests/e2e/keystone/deletion-cleanup/chainsaw-test.yaml SHALL assert finalizer presence after Ready=True, backup PushSecret NotFound after CR deletion, and absence of both KV-v2 paths in OpenBao within a 2-minute bounded timeout (CC-0079)
requirements.rationale: Regressions in finalizer behaviour or DeletionPolicy wiring must be caught in CI before release; the existing deletion-cleanup test does not verify OpenBao remote state

scenarios.name: Finalizer asserted on Ready CR
scenarios.when: the Ready=True step runs against the test Keystone CR
scenarios.then: the assertion passes when metadata.finalizers contains "keystone.openstack.c5c3.io/openbao-finalizer"

scenarios.name: PushSecrets NotFound after deletion
scenarios.when: the test deletes the Keystone CR and waits for cleanup
scenarios.then: both {name}-fernet-keys-backup and {name}-credential-keys-backup PushSecrets are NotFound within 2 minutes
and_then: the Keystone CR itself is NotFound within the same timeout

scenarios.name: OpenBao KV-v2 paths are empty
scenarios.when: after the PushSecrets are NotFound, a script step runs kubectl exec against the openbao pod with bao kv get for each path
scenarios.then: bao kv get kv-v2/openstack/keystone/fernet-keys exits non-zero (path not found)
and_then: bao kv get kv-v2/openstack/keystone/credential-keys exits non-zero

requirements.description: The reference documentation (docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md) SHALL describe the OpenBao finalizer: name, lifecycle, PushSecret DeletionPolicy integration, failure modes, and idempotency (CC-0079)
requirements.rationale: Reference docs are the Diataxis quadrant for information-oriented content; finalizer behaviour is part of the controller's public contract and must be documented

scenarios.name: Reference doc lists finalizer name
scenarios.when: a reader searches docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md for "openbao-finalizer"
scenarios.then: they find the constant value "keystone.openstack.c5c3.io/openbao-finalizer" and the Go file where it is defined

scenarios.name: Reference doc describes DeletionPolicy flow
scenarios.when: a reader asks how the remote KV-v2 paths are actually deleted
scenarios.then: the doc explains PushSecret Spec.DeletionPolicy=Delete and that ESO performs the remote delete
and_then: the doc names both backup PushSecrets and the KV-v2 paths they target

scenarios.name: Reference doc notes OpenBao-unavailable failure mode
scenarios.when: a reader looks up what happens when OpenBao is unreachable
scenarios.then: the doc explains SecretsReady=False/OpenBaoFinalizerBlocked and the RequeueSecretPolling loop

tasks.title: Define package-level constant keystoneOpenBaoFinalizer = "keystone.openstack.c5c3.io/openbao-finalizer" in operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_secrets.go and add unit test TestKeystoneOpenBaoFinalizerConstant_HasExpectedValue in keystone_controller_test.go (REQ-005, CC-0079)

Set Spec.DeletionPolicy = esov1alpha1.PushSecretDeletionPolicyDelete in fernetKeysPushSecret in operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_fernet.go and add unit test TestFernetKeysPushSecret_HasDeletionPolicyDelete in reconcile_secrets_test.go (REQ-008, CC-0079)

Set Spec.DeletionPolicy = esov1alpha1.PushSecretDeletionPolicyDelete in credentialKeysPushSecret in operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_credential.go and add unit test TestCredentialKeysPushSecret_HasDeletionPolicyDelete in reconcile_secrets_test.go (REQ-008, CC-0079)

Extend reconcile_fernet_test.go with TestReconcileFernetKeys_PushSecretDeletionPolicyDelete and reconcile_credential_test.go with TestReconcileCredentialKeys_PushSecretDeletionPolicyDelete: run the sub-reconciler, Get the stored PushSecret from fake client, assert DeletionPolicy=Delete (REQ-008, CC-0079)

Implement finalizeOpenBaoSecrets(ctx, keystone) in operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_secrets.go: issue client.Delete(IgnoreNotFound) for {name}-fernet-keys-backup and {name}-credential-keys-backup PushSecrets; Get each afterwards; return (done bool, stuckName string, err error) where done=true only when both are confirmed NotFound; tolerate apierrors.IsNotFound on Delete and Get (REQ-002, REQ-003, REQ-004, CC-0079)

Add unit tests in reconcile_secrets_test.go for finalizeOpenBaoSecrets: TestFinalizeOpenBaoSecrets_DeletesBothPushSecrets, TestFinalizeOpenBaoSecrets_NotFoundIsTolerated, TestFinalizeOpenBaoSecrets_IsIdempotent, TestFinalizeOpenBaoSecrets_RequeuesWhilePushSecretTerminating; use fake.NewClientBuilder with esov1alpha1 scheme; use fake client interceptor to count Delete/Get calls for idempotency assertion (REQ-002, REQ-003, REQ-004, CC-0079)

Extend finalizeOpenBaoSecrets (or add setOpenBaoFinalizerBlockedCondition helper) to set SecretsReady=False, reason="OpenBaoFinalizerBlocked", message naming the stuck PushSecret; write only when done=false; covered by TestFinalizeOpenBaoSecrets_SetsBlockedConditionOnStall (REQ-009, CC-0079)

In Reconcile in operators/keystone/internal/controller/keystone_controller.go, after the CR Get, branch on !keystone.DeletionTimestamp.IsZero(): if finalizer present, emit Normal "FinalizingOpenBaoSecrets" event exactly once (dedupe by checking that at least one PushSecret still exists), call finalizeOpenBaoSecrets; on done=false return ctrl.Result{RequeueAfter: RequeueSecretPolling} via updateStatus; on done=true emit Normal "OpenBaoSecretsFinalized", RemoveFinalizer, Update; if finalizer absent, return empty result (REQ-002, REQ-006, REQ-007, CC-0079)

In the same Reconcile function, add a pre-sub-reconciler block (live CR path) that calls controllerutil.AddFinalizer(keystone, keystoneOpenBaoFinalizer) when missing and returns ctrl.Result{Requeue: true} after a successful client.Update so the next pass runs with the finalizer persisted; leave the existing sub-reconciler pipeline unchanged when the finalizer is already present (REQ-001, REQ-006, CC-0079)

Add unit tests in keystone_controller_test.go for finalizer add/remove flow: TestReconcile_AddsOpenBaoFinalizerOnFirstReconcile, TestReconcile_OpenBaoFinalizerAlreadyPresent_NoExtraUpdate, TestReconcile_OpenBaoFinalizerUpdateConflict_ReturnsError (inject Conflict via interceptor), TestReconcile_TerminatingCR_SkipsSubReconcilers, TestReconcile_TerminatingCR_RequeuesWhilePushSecretsExist, TestReconcile_TerminatingCR_WithoutOpenBaoFinalizer_NoOp (REQ-001, REQ-002, REQ-004, REQ-006, CC-0079)

Add unit tests in keystone_controller_test.go for event emission: TestReconcile_TerminatingCR_EmitsFinalizingOpenBaoSecretsEvent, TestReconcile_TerminatingCR_EmitsOpenBaoSecretsFinalizedEvent, TestReconcile_TerminatingCR_NoDuplicateStartEventOnRequeue; use record.NewFakeRecorder(10) and drain the channel to assert reason strings (REQ-007, CC-0079)

Add integration test TestIntegration_OpenBaoFinalizerLifecycle_AddAndRemove in operators/keystone/internal/controller/integration_test.go using envtest: create a managed Keystone CR; Eventually assert keystoneOpenBaoFinalizer present; verify both backup PushSecrets exist with DeletionPolicy=Delete; delete the CR; simulate ESO clearing its finalizer on each PushSecret so the API server garbage-collects them; Eventually assert PushSecrets are NotFound and the Keystone CR itself is NotFound within a bounded timeout (REQ-002, REQ-008, CC-0079)

Add integration test TestIntegration_OpenBaoFinalizer_BlockedWhenPushSecretStuck in integration_test.go: create Keystone CR, delete it, leave the ESO finalizer on fernet-keys-backup (do not clear); Eventually assert status.conditions contains SecretsReady=False with reason OpenBaoFinalizerBlocked and message naming fernet-keys-backup; then clear the ESO finalizer; Eventually assert the Keystone CR is NotFound (REQ-004, REQ-009, CC-0079)

Extend tests/e2e/keystone/deletion-cleanup/chainsaw-test.yaml: (a) in the Ready=True step add an assertion that metadata.finalizers contains "keystone.openstack.c5c3.io/openbao-finalizer"; (b) keep the existing PushSecret error assertion for fernet-keys-backup and ADD an error assertion for {name}-credential-keys-backup after deletion; (c) ensure delete step uses a <=2m bounded timeout (REQ-010, CC-0079)

Add a final script step to tests/e2e/keystone/deletion-cleanup/chainsaw-test.yaml that uses kubectl exec against the openbao pod (namespace openbao, label app.kubernetes.io/name=openbao) to run `bao kv get kv-v2/openstack/keystone/fernet-keys` and `bao kv get kv-v2/openstack/keystone/credential-keys`; script asserts both commands exit non-zero (path not found) and prints the output for debugging (REQ-010, CC-0079)

review_criteria: BLOCKER: Reconcile correctly branches on DeletionTimestamp before invoking any sub-reconciler (REQ-006); verified by TestReconcile_TerminatingCR_SkipsSubReconcilers
BLOCKER: The openbao finalizer constant is defined once with value "keystone.openstack.c5c3.io/openbao-finalizer" (REQ-005); grep confirms no duplicate Go string literals

BLOCKER: Both fernetKeysPushSecret and credentialKeysPushSecret set Spec.DeletionPolicy to PushSecretDeletionPolicyDelete (REQ-008); verified by unit tests and visible in kubectl get pushsecret -o yaml

BLOCKER: finalizeOpenBaoSecrets returns done=true only when both backup PushSecrets are confirmed NotFound on a fresh Get after Delete (REQ-002, REQ-004); apierrors.IsNotFound is tolerated on every call (REQ-003)
BLOCKER: The openbao finalizer is removed via controllerutil.RemoveFinalizer + client.Update only after done=true (REQ-002)
BLOCKER: All new unit tests, integration tests, and the extended chainsaw E2E test for CC-0079 pass in CI; go vet and golangci-lint produce no new findings
CRITICAL: CRs without the openbao finalizer are not blocked on deletion (REQ-002); TestReconcile_TerminatingCR_WithoutOpenBaoFinalizer_NoOp passes
CRITICAL: While a PushSecret is stuck in Terminating state, SecretsReady=False with reason OpenBaoFinalizerBlocked is surfaced (REQ-009); confirmed by unit and integration tests
CRITICAL: Events "FinalizingOpenBaoSecrets" (start) and "OpenBaoSecretsFinalized" (completion) are emitted exactly once each per deletion (REQ-007); confirmed by record.FakeRecorder assertions
CRITICAL: The chainsaw E2E test actually verifies OpenBao KV-v2 paths are absent via kubectl exec + bao kv get (REQ-010), not only PushSecret NotFound
MINOR: Reference docs in docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md are updated (REQ-011) and name the two KV-v2 paths and both backup PushSecrets
MINOR: Every new log line, event message, condition message, and doc reference includes the feature ID "CC-0079"
MINOR: No unrelated refactoring of existing reconcileSecrets / reconcileFernetKeys / reconcileCredentialKeys logic; task scope strictly limited to finalizer and DeletionPolicy wiring
implementation_notes:
ARCHITECTURAL DECISIONS (CC-0079):

1. Finalizer name: "keystone.openstack.c5c3.io/openbao-finalizer". Dedicated to OpenBao/PushSecret cleanup, kept separate from any MariaDB finalizer (CC-0078) so the two cleanup domains remove independently. Kubernetes permits multiple finalizers; each is stripped when its own cleanup completes. Constant lives in reconcile_secrets.go (package-level, lower-case, package-private) next to openBaoClusterStoreName.

2. Cleanup strategy: delegate the remote KV-v2 delete to ESO via PushSecret Spec.DeletionPolicy=Delete. The Keystone operator has no OpenBao credentials; ESO is the only component with the provider client. The finalizer therefore (a) issues client.Delete on the two backup PushSecrets, (b) re-Gets each to observe ESO's finalizer dance, (c) removes its own finalizer only once both PushSecrets are fully NotFound. This ensures the user-visible "Keystone CR gone" state implies the remote keys have actually been purged (or that ESO has surfaced the failure via its own status).

3. Why not delete the KV-v2 path directly from the operator? Would require embedding OpenBao credentials in the operator ServiceAccount, duplicating ESO's backend abstraction, and breaks the single-responsibility of who talks to OpenBao. The ESO-mediated path reuses the existing ClusterSecretStore and its auth.

4. Reconcile branching: finalizer handling runs immediately after the CR Get and before reconcileSecrets. A Terminating CR must not re-enter the sub-reconciler pipeline because reconcileFernetKeys/reconcileCredentialKeys would re-create the PushSecrets the finalizer just deleted — classic infinite loop.

5. done=true vs done=false: finalizeOpenBaoSecrets returns done=true only after a fresh Get confirms NotFound on both PushSecrets. Getting back a DeletionTimestamped object (ESO finalizer still present) still counts as "present" → return done=false. This guarantees we do not strand a Keystone CR in deleting state after the Keystone finalizer is stripped while ESO's finalizer is still blocking its own object.

6. Event deduplication: the "FinalizingOpenBaoSecrets" Event is emitted only when at least one PushSecret is still present on entry to the finalizer handler. Once both are already NotFound on entry (re-runs, pre-deleted PushSecrets), the start Event is skipped and only the completion Event fires. This avoids Event spam under requeue loops while still giving operators a kubectl-describe trail.

7. Status surfacing: while the finalizer is blocked on ESO, set SecretsReady=False with reason="OpenBaoFinalizerBlocked" and a message naming the stuck PushSecret. Reusing the existing SecretsReady condition (CC-0013, CC-0047) keeps the status surface coherent — any secret-store lifecycle concern appears under the same condition type.

8. Log correlation: every finalizer-related log line includes keystone.name / keystone.namespace via log.FromContext(ctx).WithValues("keystone", client.ObjectKeyFromObject(keystone).String()) per the established operator logging pattern.

9. Shared KV-v2 paths (known limitation, out of scope): both backup PushSecrets target cluster-global paths (kv-v2/data/openstack/keystone/fernet-keys, /credential-keys) rather than {name}-scoped paths. In multi-Keystone-per-cluster deployments, deleting one CR would cleanup the shared path used by all. Deferred to a separate design discussion (see deferred_items). The current deploy model is single-Keystone-per-cluster for the openstack namespace, so this is not observable in CI.

10. Interaction with CC-0078 (MariaDB finalizer): if CC-0078 has merged, both finalizers are added on the live-CR path and removed independently. Reconcile's DeletionTimestamp branch invokes both finalizer handlers in sequence (openbao first, then database — order does not matter for correctness). If CC-0078 has not merged, the openbao finalizer stands alone. No coupling between the two code paths other than sharing the same Reconcile entry point.

11. No schema changes. metadata.finalizers is a built-in array on every object; Spec.DeletionPolicy is an existing field on PushSecret.

DATA MODEL:
- Constant: keystoneOpenBaoFinalizer string = "keystone.openstack.c5c3.io/openbao-finalizer" (reconcile_secrets.go)
- PushSecret field: Spec.DeletionPolicy = esov1alpha1.PushSecretDeletionPolicyDelete on both backups
- Condition: SecretsReady=False, reason="OpenBaoFinalizerBlocked", message="PushSecret <name> is still Terminating; ESO may be unable to reach OpenBao"

ARCHITECTURE DIAGRAM:

  +------------------+       +---------------------------+
  |  kubectl delete  | ----> |  Keystone CR              |
  |  keystone <name> |       |  (DeletionTimestamp set)  |
  +------------------+       +---------------------------+
                                          |
                                          v
                             +---------------------------+
                             | KeystoneReconciler.       |
                             | Reconcile()               |
                             |  └─ if DeletionTS != 0 && |
                             |     openbao finalizer set  |
                             +---------------------------+
                                          |
                                          v
                             +---------------------------+
                             | finalizeOpenBaoSecrets    |
                             |  ─ Delete(fernet-backup) ─+─> ESO finalizer held
                             |  ─ Delete(cred-backup)   ─+─> ESO deletes KV-v2 path
                             |  ─ re-Get each           |      │
                             +---------------------------+      v
                                          |            OpenBao /kv-v2/openstack/keystone/*
                               both NotFound?                   wiped
                                  yes /          \ no
                                     v            v
                     RemoveFinalizer +Update    Set SecretsReady=False/OpenBaoFinalizerBlocked;
                                     |          Requeue RequeueSecretPolling
                                     v
                             API server deletes Keystone CR

DATA FLOW:
  1. User issues kubectl delete keystone <name>.
  2. API server sets DeletionTimestamp; Keystone CR stays in etcd because openbao-finalizer is present.
  3. Watch triggers Reconcile.
  4. Reconcile Gets the CR, sees DeletionTimestamp != 0, skips sub-reconcilers.
  5. On first pass where at least one PushSecret is still present: emit "FinalizingOpenBaoSecrets" Event.
  6. Call finalizeOpenBaoSecrets: Delete(IgnoreNotFound) on each backup PushSecret.
  7. Each PushSecret has DeletionPolicy=Delete; ESO picks up the DeletionTimestamp, purges the KV-v2 path in OpenBao, removes its own finalizer, API server deletes the PushSecret.
  8. Re-Get each PushSecret: if any still present, set SecretsReady=False/OpenBaoFinalizerBlocked and requeue after RequeueSecretPolling.
  9. Next reconcile: re-run finalizeOpenBaoSecrets; when both NotFound, emit "OpenBaoSecretsFinalized", RemoveFinalizer, Update.
 10. API server observes empty openbao finalizer and garbage-collects the Keystone CR (once any other finalizers are also cleared).

PITFALLS TO AVOID:
- Do NOT RemoveFinalizer before confirming NotFound on both PushSecrets (would leave OpenBao paths orphaned if ESO has not yet finished).
- Do NOT talk to OpenBao directly from the operator (credentials, lifecycle, and abstraction all live in ESO).
- Do NOT emit "FinalizingOpenBaoSecrets" on every requeue (noise under stuck deletions); check "at least one PushSecret present" sentinel on entry.
- Do NOT run sub-reconcilers on a Terminating CR (re-creates PushSecrets, infinite loop).
- Do NOT add Update-conflict error Events (controller-runtime retry backoff handles it; Events would be noise).
- Keep the finalizer constant package-private (lower-case initial); no cross-package consumer exists.
- Do NOT forget DeletionPolicy=Delete on the builder — without it, the PushSecret is deleted locally but the remote KV-v2 path stays, defeating the feature.

PATTERNS TO FOLLOW:
- Reconcile structure and updateStatus usage already defined in keystone_controller.go:79-185.
- Package-level constants live alongside related helpers in reconcile_secrets.go (see openBaoClusterStoreName:26).
- Fake client unit tests with secretsTestScheme(), secretsTestKeystone(), record.NewFakeRecorder(); interceptor.Funcs for Conflict/error injection (see reconcile_secrets_test.go).
- Integration tests follow the envtest pattern in integration_test.go (Gomega Eventually, testutil.SetupKeystoneEnvTestWithController, simulators package).
- Chainsaw step structure in tests/e2e/keystone/deletion-cleanup/chainsaw-test.yaml (apply, assert, delete, error + final script step for remote verification).
- Condition writing via conditions.SetCondition with ObservedGeneration — pattern sub-reconciler-condition-contract-with-observedgeneration.md.
- Event emission via r.Recorder.Event/Eventf paired with condition updates — pattern kubernetes-event-emission-alongside-condition-setting-with-fakerecorder-test-ass.md.

DEFERRED ITEMS:
- Per-CR-scoped KV-v2 paths (e.g. kv-v2/data/openstack/keystone/<name>/fernet-keys). Current shared-path design works for single-Keystone-per-namespace deploys; revisit if multi-Keystone-per-namespace lands.
- OpenBao-side policy verification (e.g. confirm the ESO policy actually grants delete on kv-v2/*). Currently implicit via deploy/openbao/policies/eso-management.hcl.
- Grace-period / timeout escalation when ESO is permanently wedged: today the reconciler retries forever with SecretsReady=False. Consider a DeletionTimestamp-age heuristic in a follow-up if operator teams report stuck deletions.
